verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc erat dies festus Judæorum, et ascendit Jesus Jerosolymam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ ταῦτα ἦν ἑορτὴ τῶν ἰουδαίων καὶ ἀνέβη ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα
verses.king_james: After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est autem Jerosolymis probatica piscina, quæ cognominatur hebraice Bethsaida, quinque porticus habens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔστιν δὲ ἐν τοῖς ἱεροσολύμοις ἐπὶ τῇ προβατικῇ κολυμβήθρα ἡ ἐπιλεγομένη ἑβραϊστὶ βηθεσδά, πέντε στοὰς ἔχουσα
verses.king_james: Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep *market* a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.

verses.latin_vulgate: In his jacebat multitudo magna languentium, cæcorum, claudorum, aridorum, exspectantium aquæ motum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ταύταις κατέκειτο πλῆθος πολὺ τῶν ἀσθενούντων τυφλῶν χωλῶν ξηρῶν ἐκδεχομένων τὴν τοῦ ὕδατος κίνησιν
verses.king_james: In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Angelus autem Domini descendebat secundum tempus in piscinam, et movebatur aqua. Et qui prior descendisset in piscinam post motionem aquæ, sanus fiebat a quacumque detinebatur infirmitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄγγελος γὰρ κατὰ καιρὸν κατέβαινεν ἐν τῇ κολυμβήθρᾳ, καὶ ἐτάρασσεν τὸ ὕδωρ ὁ οὖν πρῶτος ἐμβὰς μετὰ τὴν ταραχὴν τοῦ ὕδατος, ὑγιὴς ἐγίνετο, ᾧ δήποτε κατειχετο νοσήματι
verses.king_james: For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem quidam homo ibi triginta et octo annos habens in infirmitate sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δέ τις ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖ τριακονταοκτώ ἔτη ἔχων ἐν τῇ ἀσθενείᾳ
verses.king_james: And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hunc autem cum vidisset Jesus jacentem, et cognovisset quia jam multum tempus haberet, dicit ei: Vis sanus fieri?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτον ἰδὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς κατακείμενον καὶ γνοὺς ὅτι πολὺν ἤδη χρόνον ἔχει λέγει αὐτῷ θέλεις ὑγιὴς γενέσθαι
verses.king_james: When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time *in that case*, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ei languidus: Domine, hominem non habeo, ut, cum turbata fuerit aqua, mittat me in piscinam: dum venio enim ego, alius ante me descendit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ ὁ ἀσθενῶν κύριε ἄνθρωπον οὐκ ἔχω ἵνα ὅταν ταραχθῇ τὸ ὕδωρ βάλλῃ με εἰς τὴν κολυμβήθραν ἐν ᾧ δὲ ἔρχομαι ἐγὼ ἄλλος πρὸ ἐμοῦ καταβαίνει
verses.king_james: The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Surge, tolle grabatum tuum et ambula.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐγεῖραι ἆρον τὸν κράββατον σου καὶ περιπάτει
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim sanus factus est homo ille: et sustulit grabatum suum, et ambulabat. Erat autem sabbatum in die illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως ἐγένετο ὑγιὴς ὁ ἄνθρωπος καὶ ἦρεν τὸν κράββατον αὐτοῦ καὶ περιεπάτει ἦν δὲ σάββατον ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ
verses.king_james: And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo Judæi illi qui sanatus fuerat: Sabbatum est, non licet tibi tollere grabatum tuum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι τῷ τεθεραπευμένῳ σάββατόν ἐστιν οὐκ ἔξεστίν σοι ἆραι τὸν κράββατον
verses.king_james: The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry *thy* bed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis: Qui me sanum fecit, ille mihi dixit: Tolle grabatum tuum et ambula.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ποιήσας με ὑγιῆ ἐκεῖνός μοι εἰπεν ἆρον τὸν κράββατον σου καὶ περιπάτει
verses.king_james: He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interrogaverunt ergo eum: Quis est ille homo qui dixit tibi: Tolle grabatum tuum et ambula?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠρώτησαν οὖν αὐτόν τίς ἐστιν ὁ ἄνθρωπος ὁ εἰπών σοι ἆρον τὸν κράββατον σου καὶ περιπάτει
verses.king_james: Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?

verses.latin_vulgate: Is autem qui sanus fuerat effectus, nesciebat quis esset. Jesus enim declinavit a turba constituta in loco.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰαθεὶς οὐκ ᾔδει τίς ἐστιν ὁ γὰρ ἰησοῦς ἐξένευσεν ὄχλου ὄντος ἐν τῷ τόπῳ
verses.king_james: And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in *that* place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postea invenit eum Jesus in templo, et dixit illi: Ecce sanus factus es; jam noli peccare, ne deterius tibi aliquid contingat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ ταῦτα εὑρίσκει αὐτὸν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἴδε ὑγιὴς γέγονας μηκέτι ἁμάρτανε ἵνα μὴ χεῖρόν τι σοί γένηται
verses.king_james: Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit ille homo, et nuntiavit Judæis quia Jesus esset, qui fecit eum sanum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπῆλθεν ὁ ἄνθρωπος καὶ ἀνήγγειλεν τοῖς ἰουδαίοις ὅτι ἰησοῦς ἐστιν ὁ ποιήσας αὐτὸν ὑγιῆ
verses.king_james: The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea persequebantur Judæi Jesum, quia hæc faciebat in sabbato.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διὰ τοῦτο ἐδίωκον τὸν ἰησοῦν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι καὶ ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν ἀποκτεῖναι, ὅτι ταῦτα ἐποίει ἐν σαββάτῳ
verses.king_james: And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem respondit eis: Pater meus usque modo operatur, et ego operor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἀπεκρίνατο αὐτοῖς ὁ πατήρ μου ἕως ἄρτι ἐργάζεται κἀγὼ ἐργάζομαι
verses.king_james: But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea ergo magis quærebant eum Judæi interficere: quia non solum solvebat sabbatum, sed et patrem suum dicebat Deum, æqualem se faciens Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο οὖν μᾶλλον ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἀποκτεῖναι ὅτι οὐ μόνον ἔλυεν τὸ σάββατον ἀλλὰ καὶ πατέρα ἴδιον ἔλεγεν τὸν θεόν ἴσον ἑαυτὸν ποιῶν τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit itaque Jesus, et dixit eis: Amen, amen dico vobis: non potest Filius a se facere quidquam, nisi quod viderit Patrem facientem: quæcumque enim ille fecerit, hæc et Filius similiter facit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίνατο οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν οὐ δύναται ὁ υἱὸς ποιεῖν ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτοῦ οὐδὲν ἐὰν μή τι βλέπῃ τὸν πατέρα ποιοῦντα ἃ γὰρ ἂν ἐκεῖνος ποιῇ ταῦτα καὶ ὁ υἱὸς ὁμοίως ποιεῖ
verses.king_james: Then answered Jesus and said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pater enim diligit Filium, et omnia demonstrat ei quæ ipse facit: et majora his demonstrabit ei opera, ut vos miremini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ πατὴρ φιλεῖ τὸν υἱὸν καὶ πάντα δείκνυσιν αὐτῷ ἃ αὐτὸς ποιεῖ καὶ μείζονα τούτων δείξει αὐτῷ ἔργα ἵνα ὑμεῖς θαυμάζητε
verses.king_james: For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim Pater suscitat mortuos, et vivificat, sic et Filius, quos vult, vivificat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ γὰρ ὁ πατὴρ ἐγείρει τοὺς νεκροὺς καὶ ζῳοποιεῖ οὕτως καὶ ὁ υἱὸς οὓς θέλει ζῳοποιεῖ
verses.king_james: For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth *them*; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque enim Pater judicat quemquam: sed omne judicium dedit Filio,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ γὰρ ὁ πατὴρ κρίνει οὐδένα ἀλλὰ τὴν κρίσιν πᾶσαν δέδωκεν τῷ υἱῷ
verses.king_james: For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut omnes honorificent Filium, sicut honorificant Patrem; qui non honorificat Filium, non honorificat Patrem, qui misit illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα πάντες τιμῶσίν τὸν υἱὸν καθὼς τιμῶσίν τὸν πατέρα ὁ μὴ τιμῶν τὸν υἱὸν οὐ τιμᾷ τὸν πατέρα τὸν πέμψαντα αὐτόν
verses.king_james: That all *men* should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen, amen dico vobis, quia qui verbum meum audit, et credit ei qui misit me, habet vitam æternam, et in judicium non venit, sed transiit a morte in vitam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ὁ τὸν λόγον μου ἀκούων καὶ πιστεύων τῷ πέμψαντί με ἔχει ζωὴν αἰώνιον καὶ εἰς κρίσιν οὐκ ἔρχεται ἀλλὰ μεταβέβηκεν ἐκ τοῦ θανάτου εἰς τὴν ζωήν
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen, amen dico vobis, quia venit hora, et nunc est, quando mortui audient vocem Filii Dei: et qui audierint, vivent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἔρχεται ὥρα καὶ νῦν ἐστιν ὅτε οἱ νεκροὶ ἀκούσονται τῆς φωνῆς τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ οἱ ἀκούσαντες ζήσονται
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim Pater habet vitam in semetipso, sic dedit et Filio habere vitam in semetipso:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ γὰρ ὁ πατὴρ ἔχει ζωὴν ἐν ἑαυτῷ οὕτως ἔδωκεν καὶ τῷ υἱῷ ζωὴν ἔχειν ἐν ἑαυτῷ
verses.king_james: For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;

verses.latin_vulgate: et potestatem dedit ei judicium facere, quia Filius hominis est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξουσίαν ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ καὶ κρίσιν ποιεῖν ὅτι υἱὸς ἀνθρώπου ἐστίν
verses.king_james: And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite mirari hoc, quia venit hora in qua omnes qui in monumentis sunt audient vocem Filii Dei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ θαυμάζετε τοῦτο ὅτι ἔρχεται ὥρα ἐν ᾗ πάντες οἱ ἐν τοῖς μνημείοις ἀκούσονται τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,

verses.latin_vulgate: et procedent qui bona fecerunt, in resurrectionem vitæ; qui vero mala egerunt, in resurrectionem judicii.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκπορεύσονται οἱ τὰ ἀγαθὰ ποιήσαντες εἰς ἀνάστασιν ζωῆς οἱ δὲ τὰ φαῦλα πράξαντες εἰς ἀνάστασιν κρίσεως
verses.king_james: And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non possum ego a meipso facere quidquam. Sicut audio, judico: et judicium meum justum est, quia non quæro voluntatem meam, sed voluntatem ejus qui misit me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ δύναμαι ἐγὼ ποιεῖν ἀπ᾽ ἐμαυτοῦ οὐδέν καθὼς ἀκούω κρίνω καὶ ἡ κρίσις ἡ ἐμὴ δικαία ἐστίν ὅτι οὐ ζητῶ τὸ θέλημα τὸ ἐμὸν ἀλλὰ τὸ θέλημα τοῦ πέμψαντός με πατρός
verses.king_james: I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because i seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ego testimonium perhibeo de meipso, testimonium meum non est verum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν ἐγὼ μαρτυρῶ περὶ ἐμαυτοῦ ἡ μαρτυρία μου οὐκ ἔστιν ἀληθής
verses.king_james: If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alius est qui testimonium perhibet de me: et scio quia verum est testimonium, quod perhibet de me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλος ἐστὶν ὁ μαρτυρῶν περὶ ἐμοῦ καὶ οἶδα ὅτι ἀληθής ἐστιν ἡ μαρτυρία ἣν μαρτυρεῖ περὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos misistis ad Joannem, et testimonium perhibuit veritati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς ἀπεστάλκατε πρὸς ἰωάννην καὶ μεμαρτύρηκεν τῇ ἀληθείᾳ
verses.king_james: Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem non ab homine testimonium accipio: sed hæc dico ut vos salvi sitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ οὐ παρὰ ἀνθρώπου τὴν μαρτυρίαν λαμβάνω ἀλλὰ ταῦτα λέγω ἵνα ὑμεῖς σωθῆτε
verses.king_james: But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ille erat lucerna ardens et lucens: vos autem voluistis ad horam exsultare in luce ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖνος ἦν ὁ λύχνος ὁ καιόμενος καὶ φαίνων ὑμεῖς δὲ ἠθελήσατε αγαλλιασθῆναι πρὸς ὥραν ἐν τῷ φωτὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem habeo testimonium majus Joanne. Opera enim quæ dedit mihi Pater ut perficiam ea: ipsa opera, quæ ego facio, testimonium perhibent de me, quia Pater misit me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ ἔχω τὴν μαρτυρίαν μείζω τοῦ ἰωάννου τὰ γὰρ ἔργα ἃ ἔδωκεν μοι ὁ πατὴρ ἵνα τελειώσω αὐτά αὐτὰ τὰ ἔργα ἃ ἐγὼ ποιῶ μαρτυρεῖ περὶ ἐμοῦ ὅτι ὁ πατήρ με ἀπέσταλκεν
verses.king_james: But I have greater witness than *that* of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui misit me Pater, ipse testimonium perhibuit de me: neque vocem ejus umquam audistis, neque speciem ejus vidistis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ πέμψας με πατὴρ αὐτὸς μεμαρτύρηκεν περὶ ἐμοῦ οὔτε φωνὴν αὐτοῦ ἀκηκόατε πώποτε οὔτε εἶδος αὐτοῦ ἑωράκατε
verses.king_james: And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape.

verses.latin_vulgate: et verbum ejus non habetis in vobis manens: quia quem misit ille, huic vos non creditis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὸν λόγον αὐτοῦ οὐκ ἔχετε μένοντα ἐν ὑμῖν ὅτι ὃν ἀπέστειλεν ἐκεῖνος τούτῳ ὑμεῖς οὐ πιστεύετε
verses.king_james: And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scrutamini Scripturas, quia vos putatis in ipsis vitam æternam habere: et illæ sunt quæ testimonium perhibent de me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐρευνᾶτε τὰς γραφάς ὅτι ὑμεῖς δοκεῖτε ἐν αὐταῖς ζωὴν αἰώνιον ἔχειν καὶ ἐκεῖναί εἰσιν αἱ μαρτυροῦσαι περὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: et non vultis venire ad me ut vitam habeatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐ θέλετε ἐλθεῖν πρός με ἵνα ζωὴν ἔχητε
verses.king_james: And ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Claritatem ab hominibus non accipio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δόξαν παρὰ ἀνθρώπων οὐ λαμβάνω
verses.king_james: I receive not honour from men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed cognovi vos, quia dilectionem Dei non habetis in vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἔγνωκα ὑμᾶς ὅτι τὴν ἀγάπην τοῦ θεοῦ οὐκ ἔχετε ἐν ἑαυτοῖς
verses.king_james: But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego veni in nomine Patris mei, et non accipitis me; si alius venerit in nomine suo, illum accipietis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ ἐλήλυθα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ πατρός μου καὶ οὐ λαμβάνετέ με ἐὰν ἄλλος ἔλθῃ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τῷ ἰδίῳ ἐκεῖνον λήψεσθε
verses.king_james: I am come in my Father's name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo vos potestis credere, qui gloriam ab invicem accipitis, et gloriam quæ a solo Deo est, non quæritis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πῶς δύνασθε ὑμεῖς πιστεῦσαι δόξαν παρὰ ἀλλήλων λαμβάνοντες καὶ τὴν δόξαν τὴν παρὰ τοῦ μόνου θεοῦ οὐ ζητεῖτε
verses.king_james: How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that *cometh* from God only?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite putare quia ego accusaturus sim vos apud Patrem: est qui accusat vos Moyses, in quo vos speratis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ δοκεῖτε ὅτι ἐγὼ κατηγορήσω ὑμῶν πρὸς τὸν πατέρα ἔστιν ὁ κατηγορῶν ὑμῶν μωσῆς, εἰς ὃν ὑμεῖς ἠλπίκατε
verses.king_james: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is *one* that accuseth you, *even* Moses, in whom ye trust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim crederetis Moysi, crederetis forsitan et mihi: de me enim ille scripsit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἐπιστεύετε μωσῇ, ἐπιστεύετε ἂν ἐμοί περὶ γὰρ ἐμοῦ ἐκεῖνος ἔγραψεν
verses.king_james: For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed me: for he wrote of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem illius litteris non creditis, quomodo verbis meis credetis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ τοῖς ἐκείνου γράμμασιν οὐ πιστεύετε πῶς τοῖς ἐμοῖς ῥήμασιν πιστεύσετε
verses.king_james: But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?
